quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Codon"?

options.text: Genes on the X chromosome; males (XY) express all X-linked alleles; conditions like hemophilia and color blindness are more common in males
A three-nucleotide sequence in mRNA that codes for a specific amino acid or stop signal; 64 total codons, 61 code for amino acids
O₂; accepts electrons at the end of the ETC and combines with H⁺ to form water; without O₂, the ETC stops
The energy available to do work; ΔG  0 (endergonic, requires energy input), ΔG = 0 (equilibrium)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Codon" is defined as: A three-nucleotide sequence in mRNA that codes for a specific amino acid or stop signal; 64 total codons, 61 code for amino acids. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "tRNA (transfer RNA)"?

options.text: A series of protein kinases that sequentially phosphorylate and activate each other, amplifying the signal at each step
The pathway by which a chemical element (C, N, P, H₂O) moves through the biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem
An adapter molecule that carries a specific amino acid and has an anticodon complementary to an mRNA codon
Multiple genes contribute to a single trait, producing continuous variation (e.g., skin color, height, intelligence)

quiz_questions.explanation: "tRNA (transfer RNA)" is defined as: An adapter molecule that carries a specific amino acid and has an anticodon complementary to an mRNA codon. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Ribosome"?

options.text: The molecular machine for translation; composed of large and small subunits containing rRNA and proteins; has A, P, and E sites
The specific region of an enzyme where the substrate binds and the catalytic reaction occurs; shape determines specificity
A shared derived character that defines a clade; evidence of common ancestry (e.g., feathers in birds)
Differential survival and reproduction of individuals with favorable heritable traits in a given environment; the primary mechanism of adaptive evolution

quiz_questions.explanation: "Ribosome" is defined as: The molecular machine for translation; composed of large and small subunits containing rRNA and proteins; has A, P, and E sites. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Start codon (AUG)"?

options.text: Cyclins accumulate and activate CDKs at specific cell cycle stages, driving progression through phosphorylation of target proteins
RNA polymerase reads the DNA template strand 3'→5' and synthesizes complementary mRNA 5'→3'; occurs in the nucleus in eukaryotes
The monomer of proteins: a central carbon bonded to an amino group (-NH₂), carboxyl group (-COOH), hydrogen, and variable R-group
The codon that initiates translation and codes for methionine; sets the reading frame for the entire mRNA

quiz_questions.explanation: "Start codon (AUG)" is defined as: The codon that initiates translation and codes for methionine; sets the reading frame for the entire mRNA. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Degeneracy of genetic code"?

options.text: Most amino acids are encoded by multiple codons (e.g., leucine has 6 codons); provides some protection against point mutations
A measure of disorder or randomness; the 2nd law of thermodynamics states that entropy of the universe increases in spontaneous processes
A 10-step metabolic pathway in the cytoplasm that converts glucose (6C) into 2 pyruvate (3C), producing 2 net ATP and 2 NADH
O₂; accepts electrons at the end of the ETC and combines with H⁺ to form water; without O₂, the ETC stops

quiz_questions.explanation: "Degeneracy of genetic code" is defined as: Most amino acids are encoded by multiple codons (e.g., leucine has 6 codons); provides some protection against point mutations. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A three-nucleotide sequence in mRNA that codes for a specific amino acid or stop signal; 64 total codons, 61 code for amino acids"?

options.text: Adaptive radiation
Law of Segregation
Missense mutation
Codon

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Codon".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "An adapter molecule that carries a specific amino acid and has an anticodon complementary to an mRNA codon"?

options.text: Genotype frequency
Allopatric speciation
tRNA (transfer RNA)
Cohesion

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "tRNA (transfer RNA)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The molecular machine for translation; composed of large and small subunits containing rRNA and proteins; has A, P, and E sites"?

options.text: Krebs cycle (citric acid cycle)
Ribosome
Denaturation
Cladogram

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Ribosome".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The codon that initiates translation and codes for methionine; sets the reading frame for the entire mRNA"?

options.text: Independent assortment
Exponential growth
Meiosis
Start codon (AUG)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Start codon (AUG)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Most amino acids are encoded by multiple codons (e.g., leucine has 6 codons); provides some protection against point mutations"?

options.text: CRISPR-Cas9
Steroid
Degeneracy of genetic code
Denaturation

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Degeneracy of genetic code".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Translation is accurate?

options.text: Enzyme activity follows Michaelis-Menten kinetics characterized by Vmax (maximum velocity) and Km (substrate concentration at half Vmax).
Adaptive radiation (rapid diversification from a single ancestor into multiple ecological niches) often follows mass extinctions or colonization of new environments.
Photosystems II and I absorb photons, exciting electrons that pass through electron transport chains.
Translation is the process by which ribosomes synthesize proteins from mRNA templates.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Translation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Translation?

options.text: Genetic mapping constructs a linear order of genes along chromosomes based on recombination frequencies.
Transfer RNA (tRNA) molecules carry amino acids and match their anticodons to mRNA codons.

Non-Mendelian Inheritance includes patterns that do not follow simple dominant-recessive rules: incomplete dominance (blending phenotypes, e.g., red × white = pink in snapdragons), codominance (both alleles fully expressed, e.g., AB blood type),...
The Knudson two-hit hypothesis explains that both copies of a tumor suppressor must be inactivated for cancer to develop.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Translation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Translation?

options.text: Enzyme specificity arises from the precise 3D shape of the active site, which is determined by the amino acid sequence.
These properties arise from water's bent molecular geometry and electronegativity difference between O and H.

Translation occurs in three stages: initiation (ribosome assembles at start codon AUG), elongation (amino acids added one by one as ribosome moves along mRNA), and termination (release factor binds stop codon).

Eukaryotic cells compartmentalize functions in membrane-bound organelles: the nucleus (DNA storage), endoplasmic reticulum (protein/lipid synthesis), Golgi apparatus (modification and sorting), mitochondria (ATP production), and chloroplasts...

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Translation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Translation?

options.text: Island biogeography theory (MacArthur and Wilson) predicts that species richness on an island reflects a balance between immigration and extinction rates, influenced by island size and distance from the mainland.
The genetic code is degenerate (multiple codons per amino acid), nearly universal, and read in non-overlapping triplets.
Logistic growth (dN/dt = rN(K-N)/K) incorporates carrying capacity K and produces S-shaped curves.
The three modes of natural selection are directional (shifts the mean), stabilizing (reduces variation around the mean), and disruptive (favors extremes).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Translation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Translation?

options.text: Biodiversity and Conservation addresses the variety of life at genetic, species, and ecosystem levels and the threats to it.

Island biogeography theory (MacArthur and Wilson) predicts that species richness on an island reflects a balance between immigration and extinction rates, influenced by island size and distance from the mainland.
Cell Structure covers the organization of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
Translation is the process by which ribosomes synthesize proteins from mRNA templates.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Translation as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Translation?

options.text: Human population growth following a roughly exponential pattern from 1 billion (1800) to 8 billion (2022), with demographic transition slowing growth in developed nations
Biofuel production engineering algae with enhanced Calvin cycle efficiency to convert CO₂ into lipids for biodiesel
Antibiotic targeting of bacterial ribosomes: tetracycline blocks the A-site, chloramphenicol inhibits peptidyl transferase—both exploit differences between prokaryotic and eukaryotic ribosomes
Active transport of ions (Na⁺/K⁺ ATPase) using ATP to pump 3 Na⁺ out and 2 K⁺ in per cycle, maintaining the resting membrane potential essential for nerve impulses

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Translation.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Translation?

options.text: Metabolic syndrome and diabetes: dysregulation of glucose metabolism affecting cellular respiration, insulin signaling, and energy storage pathways
Genetic engineering using the universal genetic code to express human insulin in E. coli bacteria, producing recombinant insulin for diabetes treatment
Virus tracking during COVID-19 pandemic using SARS-CoV-2 genome sequencing to build phylogenetic trees tracing transmission chains and identifying new variants
Cyanide poisoning blocking Complex IV of the ETC, preventing electron transfer to O₂ and halting all aerobic ATP production

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Translation.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Translation applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Ecosystem services valuation estimating that pollination by insects is worth $235-577 billion annually globally, providing economic justification for pollinator conservation
Nonsense suppressor tRNA mutations in research organisms that read through stop codons, used as tools in molecular biology research
Cancer epigenetics: abnormal DNA methylation patterns silencing tumor suppressor genes or activating oncogenes without DNA mutations
Biofuel production engineering algae with enhanced Calvin cycle efficiency to convert CO₂ into lipids for biodiesel

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Translation.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Translation?

options.text: Antibiotic targeting of bacterial ribosomes: tetracycline blocks the A-site, chloramphenicol inhibits peptidyl transferase—both exploit differences between prokaryotic and eukaryotic ribosomes
Industrial enzyme applications: proteases in detergents, amylases in food processing, and DNA polymerase in PCR for forensics and diagnostics
RT-PCR diagnostic tests detecting viral RNA (COVID-19) by reverse-transcribing RNA to DNA and amplifying specific sequences
C4 and CAM photosynthesis as evolutionary adaptations to minimize photorespiration in hot, dry environments (corn, sugarcane, cacti, pineapple)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Translation.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Translation in practice?

options.text: Rising atmospheric CO₂ levels increasing carbon fixation rates in some plants (CO₂ fertilization effect) but potentially decreasing nutritional quality of crops
Lynch syndrome (hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer) caused by inherited mutations in mismatch repair genes, increasing cancer risk
Genetic engineering using the universal genetic code to express human insulin in E. coli bacteria, producing recombinant insulin for diabetes treatment
Artificial selection in agriculture: selective breeding transforming teosinte (small, hard kernels) into modern corn over thousands of years of human-directed selection

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Translation.
